title: Fix Map.put-before-cast mass assignment ordering bug
prompt:
The changeset below attempts to force `:role` to `"user"` via `Map.put/3` BEFORE calling `cast/3`:

```elixir
def profile_update_changeset(user, attrs) do
  safe_attrs = Map.put(attrs, "role", "user")
  user
  |> cast(safe_attrs, [:email, :name, :role, :is_admin])
  |> validate_required([:email])
end
```

This does NOT work: when attrs contains `"role" => "admin"`, `Map.put(attrs, "role", "user")` overwrites it correctly — BUT since the `cast/3` allowlist still INCLUDES `:role`, a clever attacker can use atom keys (`%{role: "admin"}`) or re-override via ordering. The correct fix is to REMOVE `:role` and `:is_admin` from the cast allowlist entirely. Rewrite the function to cast only `[:email, :name]`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/accounts/profile.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — Claude often 'fixes' this via Map.put without realizing cast/3 ordering defeats it. Must remove field from allowlist.
